title: South India cotton yarn steady amid weak demand, uncertainty
content:
South India cotton yarn prices remained steady amid weak demand and thin trade in Mumbai and Tiruppur due to holiday and market uncertainty. 
Buyers stayed cautious following the ceasefire and global tensions, limiting purchases. 
Stable cotton prices and cautious mill buying further reflected subdued sentiment, though seasonal demand offered some support.

title: Indian ministry signs MoUs with Porter, Gigin to boost employment
content:
The Indian Ministry of Labour & Employment yesterday signed MoUs with SmartShift Logistics Solutions Pvt. Ltd. (Porter) and Gigin Technologies to expand employment opportunities and strengthen digital job matching through the National Career Service (NCS) portal. 
Porter aims at generating nearly 600,000 opportunities annually through the NCS platform and around 3 million opportunities by 2030.

title: UK’s Sosandar returns to profitability amid robust FY26 performance
content:
Sosandar reported FY26 revenue of £42.3 million (~$57.53 million), up 14 per cent, driven by strong online growth, with own-site sales rising 24 per cent. 
The company returned to profitability with PBT of £0.4 million (~$0.54 million) and improved margins. 
Despite slightly missing revenue expectations, performance remained solid. 
Strong third-party sales supported confidence in profitable growth.

title: Ireland’s Primark boosts circular fashion with updated strategy
content:
Primark has launched Circular Product Standard 2.0 to expand circular fashion across its range. 
The framework focuses on durability, sustainable materials and recyclability, covering nine product categories. 
In FY 24/25, 5 per cent of products were circular by design, including 20 per cent of jersey. 
A new ‘Progressive’ level raises standards, backed by training and practical design improvements.

title: Bangladesh forms cabinet committee to adjust power prices amid crisis
content:
Bangladesh recently formed a six-member cabinet committee to recommend the necessary power tariff adjustment at the wholesale and retail levels amid the Middle East crisis, global oil price volatility and surging power generation costs. 
The committee will examine the necessity of revising electricity tariffs in light of global instability affecting the domestic energy sector.

title: Sri Lanka fabric imports rise modestly in early 2026 recovery
content:
Sri Lanka’s fabric imports signal a cautious, inventory-led recovery rather than a demand surge.
Supplier consolidation is intensifying, with China gaining share on pricing strength while India holds steady through proximity.
Declining shares of Taiwan and Pakistan reflect competitive pressures.
The outlook remains tied to export demand from the US and EU.

title: Functional Fabric Fair Spring 2026 concludes with strong turnout
content:
Functional Fabric Fair Spring 2026 in Portland drew strong global participation, showcasing next-generation sustainable textiles, biobased materials and advanced fabric innovations. 
With 300+ suppliers and major brands attending, the event highlighted practical, scalable solutions, industry collaboration and growing momentum towards circular, high-performance apparel.

title: India’s March WPI inflation 4.91% for textile manufacturing, RMG 1.81%
content:
India's wholesale price inflation (WPI) rose to a 38-month high of 3.88 per cent in March, provisional data show. 
Manufactured products inflation rose to 3.39 per cent from 2.92 per cent in February. 
The WPI inflation for manufacturing of textiles rose to 4.91 per cent in March from February’s 3.29 per cent. 
The same for wearing apparel dropped to 1.81 per cent in March from February’s 2.14 per cent.

title: Tariffs weigh on The Children’s Place Q4 profits
content: The Children’s Place Inc., announced what its CEO called a “disappointing” set of Q4 results with net sales sinking and losses widening.
